name: RAPPstore — official
description: Public catalog of rapplications. Each rapplication is a converged single-file agent.py produced by the double-jump loop. Pull into your local brainstem's agents/ directory and it works. Optional .egg pack restores example workflow state.
install_protocol: drop the singleton .py file into your local brainstem's `agents/` dir. Then POST /api/swarm/deploy with a bundle that includes it, OR: simply hot-load it from disk if your brainstem watches the agents/ folder. The file is everything — no install script, no pip, no native deps.

rapplications.name: BookFactory
rapplications.summary: Five-persona content pipeline (Writer → Editor [3 specialists] → CEO [2 specialists] → Publisher → Reviewer). One call, one chapter back. Best for: engineering writeups, blog posts, briefing docs, narrative content under 1500 words.
rapplications.tagline: Source material in, publishable chapter out — 8 LLM calls under one POST.
rapplications.egg_note: No pre-populated state .egg shipped — BookFactory is stateless per call. Output artifacts are written to the workspace dir specified per call.

rapplications.name: BookFactory (with Kody+Molly demo state)
rapplications.summary: Five-persona content pipeline (Writer → Editor [3 specialists] → CEO [2 specialists] → Publisher → Reviewer). One call, one chapter back. Best for: engineering writeups, blog posts, briefing docs, narrative content under 1500 words. · This demo edition ships with a pre-packaged .egg containing the Kody+Molly book-factory workspaces from cycle 3, so you can unpack it with `bash hippocampus/twin-egg.sh unpack` and immediately see what a real run produces — source material, draft, edited prose, CEO note, final chapter, reviewer score.
rapplications.tagline: BookFactory + a worked Kody/Molly run packed as one .egg you can unpack and inspect.
rapplications.egg_note: Contains 6 twin workspaces (kody, molly, writer, editor, publisher, reviewer) plus the in-flight book-factory artifacts under .shared/. Unpack with `bash hippocampus/twin-egg.sh unpack bookfactoryagent-demo.egg --into ~/.rapp-twins`. Restoration is byte-identical and SHA-256-verified.
rapplications.install_after_unpack: Already-hatched swarms come with the .egg. Start any twin: bash hippocampus/twin-sim.sh start kody. Then call BookFactory through that twin's brainstem to see a complete worked example.

publish_your_own.summary: Author a rapplication using the multi-file source pattern (see blog/89). Run the double-jump loop until convergence (typically 1-3 cycles per content shape). Collapse to a singleton via tools/build-rapplication.py. Submit a PR adding an entry to store/index.json.
